error.verbose: Limiting analysis of branches. Use --check-level=exhaustive to analyze all branches.

error.verbose: Potentially invalid type conversion in old-style C cast, clarify/fix with C++ cast

error.verbose: Limiting analysis of branches. Use --check-level=exhaustive to analyze all branches.

error.msg: Parameter 'filter' can be declared as pointer to const
error.verbose: Parameter 'filter' can be declared as pointer to const

location.info: Parameter 'filter' can be declared as pointer to const

error.msg: Limiting analysis of branches. Use --check-level=exhaustive to analyze all branches.
error.verbose: Limiting analysis of branches. Use --check-level=exhaustive to analyze all branches.

error.msg: Using memset() on struct which contains a floating point number.
error.verbose: Using memset() on struct which contains a floating point number. This is not portable because memset() sets each byte of a block of memory to a specific value and the actual representation of a floating-point value is implementation defined. Note: In case of an IEEE754-1985 compatible implementation setting all bits to zero results in the value 0.0.

error.verbose: Using memset() on struct which contains a floating point number. This is not portable because memset() sets each byte of a block of memory to a specific value and the actual representation of a floating-point value is implementation defined. Note: In case of an IEEE754-1985 compatible implementation setting all bits to zero results in the value 0.0.

error.msg: The scope of the variable 'errors' can be reduced.
error.verbose: The scope of the variable 'errors' can be reduced. Warning: Be careful when fixing this message, especially when there are inner loops. Here is an example where cppcheck will write that the scope for 'i' can be reduced:\012void f(int x)\012{\012 int i = 0;\012 if (x) {\012 // it's safe to move 'int i = 0;' here\012 for (int n = 0; n < 10; ++n) {\012 // it is possible but not safe to move 'int i = 0;' here\012 do_something(&i);\012 }\012 }\012}\012When you see this message it is always safe to reduce the variable scope 1 level.

error.msg: Function parameter 'origin' should be passed by const reference.
error.verbose: Parameter 'origin' is passed by value. It could be passed as a const reference which is usually faster and recommended in C++.

location.info: Function parameter 'origin' should be passed by const reference.

error.verbose: Parameter 'destination' is passed by value. It could be passed as a const reference which is usually faster and recommended in C++.
